content.text:
{
  "module": "document-management",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 32,
    "domain_depth": 35,
    "data_richness": 28,
    "interaction_quality": 25,
    "ai_integration": 22,
    "mobile_first": 20,
    "performance": 30,
    "typescript_quality": 45,
    "error_handling": 35,
    "realtime": 28,
    "accessibility": 18,
    "security": 40,
    "differentiation": 25,
    "enterprise_readiness": 30
  },
  "overall_score": 30,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "document-management-C7-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Drawing viewer lacks fabric.js markup annotation layer per P0-3 spec",
      "file": "src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 9 requires 7 markup tools (pen, highlighter, text, rectangle, circle, arrow, cloud) with undo/redo, color picker, and shared vs personal annotations. The current DrawingViewer component has no fabric.js canvas overlay, no annotation CRUD, and no markup toolbar integration. This is the P0-3 dealbreaker from PRODUCTION_ROADMAP.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx, in the DrawingViewer component: BEFORE: The component renders a basic PDF view or placeholder without any annotation capabilities. There is no fabric.js canvas overlay, no markup tool state, no annotation persistence, and no integration with MarkupToolbar.tsx. AFTER: Import fabric from 'fabric' (install if needed). Add a fabric.Canvas ref layered on top of the PDF render container using position absolute with identical dimensions. Add state for activeTool ('pen'|'highlighter'|'text'|'rectangle'|'circle'|'arrow'|'cloud'|null), activeColor (default '#E74C3C'), and strokeWidth (default 2). When activeTool changes, configure the fabric canvas freeDrawingBrush for pen/highlighter, or add event listeners for shape placement (rect, circle, arrow, cloud). For text tool, on canvas click create a fabric.IText at click position. Add an undoStack and redoStack (max 20 levels) that push canvas JSON state on every object:modified and path:created event. Expose onSave callback prop that serializes canvas to JSON for parent to persist to drawing_annotations table. Expose annotations prop (fabric JSON array) that loads existing annotations on mount via canvas.loadFromJSON. Add isEditable prop (default false) that disables all drawing when false. Ensure the canvas resizes with the container using a ResizeObserver. Add aria-label='Drawing markup canvas' to the canvas wrapper div. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C7-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "Drawings page missing revision management with upload, version history, and comparison",
      "file": "src/pages/Drawings.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 9 criterion 2 requires uploading a new version auto-increments revision number, previous revisions remain accessible, and criterion 12 requires side-by-side comparison with opacity slider. The Drawings page currently shows a list but lacks a revision upload flow, version history dropdown in the viewer, and the VersionCompare component is not wired into the page.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, in the default export component: BEFORE: The page displays drawings in a grid or list but has no 'Upload New Revision' button, no revision history panel per drawing, and no version comparison toggle. The VersionCompare component exists at src/components/drawings/VersionCompare.tsx but is never rendered. AFTER: Add an 'Upload New Revision' button (visible only when a drawing is selected and user has drawings.upload permission via usePermissions) that opens a file picker accepting PDF, DWG, DXF. On file select, call the documents API to create a new drawing_revisions row with revision_number incremented from the current max for that drawing_id. Show upload progress with a linear progress bar. After upload, refresh the drawing list. Add a 'Revision History' expandable section in the drawing detail panel (or side drawer) that lists all revisions from getDrawingRevisionHistory sorted desc by revision_number, showing revision number, date, issued_by, and change_description. Each revision row has a 'View' button to load that revision in the viewer and a 'Compare' button. When Compare is clicked on any non-current revision, render the VersionCompare component with version1 set to that revision's file_url and version2 set to the current revision's file_url. Add a comparisonMode toggle (side-by-side, overlay, slider) above the comparison view. When no drawing is selected, show the DrawingsEmptyState component. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C7-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Files page has no ARIA landmarks, keyboard navigation, or screen reader support",
      "file": "src/pages/Files.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require axe-core zero critical violations, keyboard navigability with Tab order following visual order, focus rings visible, Escape closes modals. The Files page lacks role='main', aria-label on the file list, keyboard-navigable file rows, and no aria-live region for upload status or search results count announcements.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Files.tsx, in the default export component: BEFORE: The page renders file list elements without ARIA roles, no aria-label on the main content region, file rows are not keyboard focusable (no tabIndex or role='row'), search results count is not announced to screen readers, and upload status changes are silent. AFTER: Wrap the main content area in a <main role='main' aria-label='Document management'>. Add role='grid' or role='list' to the file listing container with aria-label='Project files'. Make each file row focusable with tabIndex={0} and role='listitem' or role='row'. Add onKeyDown handler: Enter opens file preview, Delete triggers delete confirmation (if permitted). Add an aria-live='polite' region that announces search result counts when search input changes (e.g., '12 files found' or 'No files match your search'). Add aria-live='assertive' region that announces upload completion ('File uploaded successfully') or errors ('Upload failed: file too large'). Ensure all icon-only buttons (download, delete, preview) have aria-label attributes describing their action and target file name. Add visible focus ring styles using outline: '2px solid #F47820', outlineOffset: '2px' on focusable elements. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C7-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "Drawings page shows no linked RFIs, submittals, or punch items per acceptance criteria",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/documents.ts",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 9 criterion 4 requires RFI pins as numbered blue circles on drawings and submittal pins as green circles, with click to open linked item detail. The getDrawings API endpoint fetches drawings but does not join or return counts of linked RFIs, submittals, or punch items, making it impossible for the frontend to display these indicators.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/documents.ts, in the getDrawings function: BEFORE: The function queries the drawings table and drawing_revisions table but does not fetch any linked entity counts. The MappedDrawing type includes no fields for linkedRfiCount, linkedSubmittalCount, or linkedPunchCount. AFTER: After fetching drawings and revisions, perform three additional batch queries using the drawingIds array: (1) Query rfis table where drawing_id is in drawingIds, group by drawing_id, count as linkedRfiCount. Since rfis may not have a drawing_id column, alternatively query rfi_attachments or a drawing_links junction table if it exists, or add a comment noting this requires a migration to add drawing_id to rfis. (2) Similarly for submittals. (3) Similarly for punch_list_items with location_drawing_id or equivalent. For now, if these foreign keys do not exist, add the counts as 0 with a TODO comment explaining the migration needed. Update the MappedDrawing return to include linkedRfiCount: number, linkedSubmittalCount: number, linkedPunchCount: number fields. Ensure each drawing in the map step gets its counts from the lookup maps. Add the three new fields to the MappedDrawing type definition in src/types/api.ts if needed. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C7-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Drawing viewer not optimized for mobile field use with pinch-to-zoom and collapsible toolbar",
      "file": "src/components/drawings/MarkupToolbar.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 9 criterion 13 requires pinch-to-zoom, markup tools in a collapsible bottom toolbar, and quick-link to create RFI from markup. The MarkupToolbar component renders as a static horizontal bar that does not collapse on mobile, has no touch gesture handling, and has no 'Create RFI from Markup' action button.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/drawings/MarkupToolbar.tsx, in the MarkupToolbar component: BEFORE: The toolbar renders as a fixed horizontal bar with tool buttons. It does not detect mobile viewport, does not collapse, has no 'Create RFI' quick action, and tool buttons may be smaller than 44x44px touch targets. AFTER: Import useMediaQuery from src/hooks/useMediaQuery. When viewport width is below 768px, render the toolbar as a bottom-anchored bar (position fixed, bottom 0, left 0, right 0, zIndex 50, background white, borderTop '1px solid #E5E7EB', padding 8px) with a collapse/expand toggle chevron button at the top. When collapsed, show only the toggle button (16px tall). When expanded, show all tools in a horizontally scrollable row with each button minimum 44x44px (per Apple HIG touch target requirement). Add a 'Create RFI' button at the end of the toolbar with a MessageSquarePlus icon from lucide-react. When clicked, emit an onCreateRFI callback prop. On desktop (>=768px), keep the current layout but ensure all buttons are at least 36x36px. Add aria-label to each tool button describing its function (e.g., 'Pen tool', 'Rectangle shape tool'). Add role='toolbar' and aria-label='Drawing markup tools' to the container. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    "AI-powered drawing conflict detection: when a new revision is uploaded, automatically compare against the previous revision using image diff analysis and flag areas of change, cross-referencing with open RFIs in those drawing zones to surface potential conflicts before they become costly field issues",
    "Drawing QR code overlay: generate a unique QR code for each drawing sheet that field crews can scan with their phone to instantly open the current revision in the mobile viewer, eliminating the 'which version is this?' problem that costs superintendents 15 minutes per day"
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's drawing viewer supports basic markup (pen, text, shapes) but lacks AI conflict detection and has limited mobile markup capability. Fieldwire excels at task-to-plan linking with pin placement but has no revision comparison or AI analysis. PlanGrid (now part of ACC) had the best mobile drawing experience before acquisition but has stagnated. SiteSync's opportunity is combining Fieldwire-level mobile markup with AI-powered revision analysis and one-tap RFI creation from markups, which no competitor offers today.",
  "summary": "The document management module scores 30/100, held back by the critical absence of the P0-3 Drawing Viewer with Markup feature from PRODUCTION_ROADMAP. The Drawings page renders a basic list with revision history fetching but lacks the fabric.js annotation layer, markup tools, revision upload flow, and version comparison that PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 9 demands. The Files page has no ARIA landmarks or keyboard navigation, violating global accessibility standards. The API layer fetches drawings but returns no linked entity counts (RFIs, submittals, punch items), making it impossible to render the pin overlays that are a core differentiator. Mobile support is inadequate with no collapsible toolbar, no pinch-to-zoom integration, and touch targets below 44px. The highest priority action is implementing the fabric.js markup canvas in DrawingViewer.tsx, followed by wiring revision management and version comparison into the Drawings page. These two changes alone would lift the module from 30 to approximately 55."
}
